# UC-15.3.30 · People Counting Trends and Occupancy Analytics

## Description

Uses camera people counting to track foot traffic trends for space utilization and facility planning.

## Value

Uses camera people counting to track foot traffic trends for space utilization and facility planning.

## Implementation

Extract people_count metrics from camera API. Aggregate by location and time.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=meraki:api people_count=*`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Extract people_count metrics from camera API. Aggregate by location and time.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_network sourcetype="meraki:api" people_count=*
| timechart avg(people_count) as avg_occupancy by location
```

Understanding this SPL

**People Counting Trends and Occupancy Analytics** — Uses camera people counting to track foot traffic trends for space utilization and facility planning.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=meraki:api people_count=*`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Meraki Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 5580). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_network; **sourcetype**: meraki:api. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_network, sourcetype="meraki:api". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time with a separate series **by location** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the video NVR or camera management system for the same cameras and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Occupancy heat map by time of day; location comparison bar chart; trend sparkline.

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_network sourcetype="meraki:api" people_count=*
| timechart avg(people_count) as avg_occupancy by location
```

## Visualization

Occupancy heat map by time of day; location comparison bar chart; trend sparkline.
